q: You may pass another vehicle if the line dividing your lane from the lane you wish to enter is a ____ line.
explanation: Broken white lines mean you may pass or change lanes when safe. Double solid yellow means no passing in either direction.

q: When making a turn, you must ____ your speed.
explanation: Always reduce your speed before turning to maintain control and allow time to scan for hazards.

q: To prepare for anything coming up on the road ahead, you should:
options: Continually scan the entire road and all roadsides
Stare straight ahead at all times
Drive with your left foot resting lightly on the brake pedal
Maintain focus toward the middle of the road
explanation: Use the SIPDE system: Scan continuously. This is one of the most important safe driving habits.

q: You are driving on a busy street, and your vehicle’s accelerator sticks open. What should you do first?
options: Shift to neutral and steer to the side of the road
Turn on your four-way flashers only
explanation: Shift to neutral immediately to disengage the engine, then steer safely off the road and use brakes.

q: When approaching a reduced conflict intersection, you may not turn left from the main road.
explanation: True. Reduced conflict intersections (common in MN) are designed so you cannot make left turns from the main highway — you use a right turn then U-turn instead.

q: At night, it is hardest to see:
explanation: Pedestrians are hardest to see at night because they often wear dark clothing and are not illuminated like cars or signs.

q: If you stop along the road at night because of a breakdown:
options: Turn on your emergency flashers and leave low beams on
explanation: Use emergency flashers + low beams so other drivers can see you from far away.

q: Pedestrians using guide dogs or white canes must be given the right-of-way:
options: Only at intersections with traffic controls
At all times when they are crossing or about to cross
Never — they must wait for assistance
explanation: Always yield to anyone with a white cane or guide dog. They have the right-of-way everywhere.

q: Before you leave a parallel parking space, you should:
options: Look for traffic by turning your head over your shoulder
explanation: Always turn your head and look over your shoulder (blind spot check) before pulling out.

q: On a road with three or more lanes traveling in the same direction, you should stay in the ____ lane except to pass.
explanation: Stay in the right lane unless passing. The left lane is for passing only on multi-lane roads.

q: When driving through fog or heavy rain, it is best to:
options: Turn on your parking lights only
explanation: Low beams are required in fog/rain/snow. High beams reflect off the moisture and make it harder to see.

q: Preparing to smoke or smoking while driving is considered:
explanation: Any activity that takes your hands or eyes off the road is distracting. Smoking is explicitly listed as distracting in the manual.

q: What does alcohol do to your driving skills and judgment?
options: It harms both driving skills and judgment
explanation: Alcohol impairs reaction time, vision, judgment, and coordination — everything you need to drive safely.

q: A red arrow pointing to the right on a traffic light means:
options: Turn right after coming to a complete stop
Do not turn right until the light turns green
Turn right only if no pedestrians are present
explanation: A red arrow means you must wait for a green arrow or green light before turning in that direction.

q: When you enter traffic from a stop (pulling away from the curb), you should:
options: Drive slower than traffic for 200 feet
Wait for a large enough gap to reach the speed of traffic safely
Pull out immediately after the first car passes
Use your horn to warn others
explanation: You need a big enough gap so you can accelerate up to the speed of traffic without causing others to brake suddenly.

q: You should always use your emergency flashers when:
options: You are double-parked in a traffic lane
You are parked in a marked parking spot
Your vehicle has broken down on the roadway
You are driving in heavy rain
explanation: Use flashers when you are stopped on the roadway or shoulder due to a breakdown or emergency.

q: The traffic light is red, but a police officer is directing you to proceed. What should you do?
options: Ignore the officer and obey the light
explanation: A police officer’s directions always override traffic signals and signs.

q: If you are under 18 and your cell phone rings while driving, what should you do?
options: Answer it if you have a hands-free device
Answer only if it's your parent
Text the caller that you are driving
explanation: Under 18 drivers in MN have stricter rules. The safest and legal choice is to let it ring and call back later when stopped.

q: At night, if an oncoming vehicle does not dim its high beams, you should:
options: Look toward the center of the roadway
Look toward the right edge of your lane
Look toward the left edge of your lane
explanation: Look at the right edge of the road so the bright lights don't blind you and you can still see the lane.

q: Teenage drivers are more likely to be involved in a crash when:
explanation: Distraction from friends in the car is a major cause of teen crashes. The manual highlights this risk.

q: You must use your headlights:
options: On rainy, snowy, or foggy days
explanation: Minnesota law requires headlights whenever visibility is poor (less than 500 feet) or in rain, snow, sleet, hail, or fog.

q: When you want to make a right turn, your car should be positioned:
options: Near the center of the street
Close to the left side of the street
Close to the right side of the street
In the middle of the right lane
explanation: Always position yourself close to the right curb or edge before making a right turn.

q: When a traffic light turns green, should you go immediately?
options: Yes, or you could get a ticket for causing gridlock
Yes, but only after yielding to vehicles or pedestrians still in the intersection
No — always wait for the car in front of you to move first
explanation: Even on green, you must yield to anyone still legally in the intersection and make sure it's clear.

q: If you see orange construction signs and cones on a freeway, you must:
options: Be prepared for workers and equipment ahead and slow down if workers are present
Ignore them if no workers are visible
explanation: Orange = construction. Slow down, watch for workers, and fines are doubled ($300+) if workers are present.

q: What is the correct following distance in good weather conditions?
explanation: Use the 3-second rule in good conditions. Count 'one-thousand-one, one-thousand-two...' as the car ahead passes a fixed point.

q: When approaching a school bus with flashing red lights and an extended stop arm, you must:
options: Slow down and proceed with caution
Stop at least 20 feet away and remain stopped until the lights are off and arm is retracted
Pass on the right if there is room
Only stop if children are visible
explanation: This is one of the strictest rules. Violation can result in large fines and license suspension.

q: In Minnesota, the hands-free cell phone law means:
options: You can hold your phone if you're just talking
You cannot hold your phone at all while the vehicle is in motion or stopped in traffic
You can only use it for GPS
It only applies to drivers under 18
explanation: You may not hold the phone in your hand for any reason while driving or stopped in traffic. Mount it or use voice commands.

q: What does a solid white line on the pavement mean?
options: Lane changes are discouraged or prohibited
It marks the edge of the road only
explanation: Solid white lines mean you should stay in your lane. Crossing is discouraged and often illegal.

q: If you are approaching a railroad crossing with flashing red lights and lowered gates, you must:
options: Slow down and look both ways
Stop at least 10 feet from the nearest rail and remain stopped until the lights stop flashing
Proceed if no train is visible
Cross quickly before the gates fully lower
explanation: Never try to beat a train. Stop and wait until the lights stop and gates go up.

q: The legal blood alcohol concentration (BAC) limit for regular drivers in Minnesota is:
explanation: 0.08% for regular drivers. 0.04% for commercial drivers. Even below the limit, you can be charged if impaired.

q: When parking on a hill with a curb, facing downhill, you should turn your wheels:
explanation: Turn wheels toward the curb so the car will roll into the curb if the brakes fail.

q: What should you do when you see a vehicle with flashing blue or red lights approaching from behind?
options: Speed up to get out of the way
Pull to the right and stop
explanation: Pull over to the right shoulder and stop completely. This is for emergency vehicles.

q: In a roundabout, you should:
options: Yield to traffic already in the circle and drive counterclockwise
Enter without yielding if you are in a hurry
Stop in the middle of the roundabout
explanation: Always yield to vehicles already circulating. Drive counterclockwise and use your right turn signal when exiting.

q: Minnesota’s Move Over Law requires you to:
options: Only slow down for police cars
Slow down and move over one lane (if safe) when approaching any stopped emergency, tow, or maintenance vehicle with flashing lights
Ignore it if traffic is heavy
explanation: This law protects first responders and tow truck drivers. It applies to any vehicle with flashing lights on the shoulder.

q: What is the correct way to exit a parallel parking space?
options: Signal, check mirrors and blind spot, then back out slowly
explanation: Always signal, check all mirrors and blind spots, and back out slowly while watching for traffic and pedestrians.

q: If your car starts to skid on ice or snow, you should:
options: Turn the steering wheel in the opposite direction of the skid
Steer in the direction you want the front of the car to go and ease off the gas
explanation: Steer gently in the direction you want to go. Do not brake hard or overcorrect.

q: A flashing yellow light means:
options: Proceed with caution and yield to traffic already in the intersection
Speed up to clear the intersection
It is the same as a green light
explanation: Flashing yellow = caution. Slow down and be prepared to stop if necessary.

q: How far in advance should you signal before changing lanes or turning?
options: Only when you start the maneuver
explanation: Signal at least 100 feet before turning or changing lanes in Minnesota.

q: When driving near large trucks or buses, you should:
options: Stay in their blind spots as little as possible
Follow closely so they can see you in their mirrors
Pass them on the right whenever possible
Assume they can see you at all times
explanation: Large vehicles have big blind spots ('No-Zones'). Avoid riding beside them. Pass quickly and safely on the left.

q: If you are involved in a minor crash with no injuries, what should you do first?
options: Move vehicles out of traffic if safe, then exchange information and report if required
Call the police even if no one is hurt
Take photos and leave without talking to the other driver
explanation: Protect the scene, move cars if possible, exchange info (license, insurance, contact), and report to police if damage is over a certain amount or if required by law.
